qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: OAuth tokens are persisted to ~/.clasprc.json with world-readable default permissions on standard POSIX setups
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a pull/clone path traversal in remote script filename handling, while this finding is a local credential-file permission issue in FileCredentialStore token persistence.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's pull/clone remote filename traversal root cause.
evidence: GHSA-hqjg-pww4-pcgq describes unsafe path traversal during clasp pull or clone of malicious script content, allowing writes outside the project directory.
The only final qualifying finding, world_readable_clasprc_tokens, concerns repo/src/auth/file_credential_store.ts writing ~/.clasprc.json with default permissions during login or token refresh.
The session manifest lists pull_clone_remote_filename_breakout as non_qualifying_vuln, so no qualifying finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
